title: Clank & Slop — The Tape
tagline: — READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE —

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: Ukraine's drone campaign has turned Russian refining into a persistent wound. The Moscow refinery looks unlikely to restart in 2026, NORSI suspended processing after a strike, the Black Sea coast is rationing fuel, and Russia has imported gasoline from as far as India — a top crude exporter pinched at its own pumps.

Tokyo added Washington to its yen defence. Finance Minister Katayama said Japan is ready to respond to excessive moves and is in contact with US authorities, the clearest sign this cycle that intervention could be coordinated. USD/JPY sits near 161 after soft US payrolls, with disclosed intervention still zero.

US cash markets were shut for Independence Day, so the semiconductor complex has no fresh read after its 5.5% drop. China's cheap open-weight models keep pressing on the closed-model economics US chip valuations lean on; the clearest live signals came from oil and FX.

props.caption: Ukraine's refinery strikes turn a fuel exporter into a fuel importer at the margin

items.why: The desk's open call sits at p=0.40 that USD/JPY revisits its four-decade low before the 10 July Tokyo close; watch whether Tokyo's US-coordination talk deters short-sellers or invites them to test the line.

items.what: Russia · does the fuel squeeze worsen
items.why: Ukraine's refinery strikes have forced rationing and gasoline imports; watch whether Black Sea shortages deepen or Moscow's task force restores supply.
St. Petersburg is now inside Ukraine's range; watch whether the campaign moves from symbolic reach to sustained damage at the Baltic export ports of Primorsk and Ust-Luga.

items.what: Doha · does the next round convene
items.why: The US-Iran channel is paused until after the 9 July funeral of Iran's Supreme Leader; a scheduled round would test whether the oil calm rests on more than exhaustion.

items.what: Taiwan · do eastern patrols become routine
items.why: Beijing's first coast-guard patrol east of Taiwan tests whether routine 'law-enforcement' can manufacture a jurisdiction claim over the island's Pacific approaches.

open_calls.question: USD/JPY reaches 162.50 (a retest of its 40-year low)
open_calls.detail: Soft June payrolls and Tokyo's fresh US-coordination signal lean against a quick retest of the 162.84 four-decade low; but the US-Japan rate gap is intact, crowded shorts remain, and disclosed intervention is still zero, so the tail stays open.

items.what: Brent held near $72 with the forward curve in contango, the clearest sign the market has stopped pricing a Hormuz shortage as Gulf flows rebuild.
Spot gold traded near $4,174 an ounce after the soft US payrolls print, on track for its first weekly gain after four weeks of declines.
China's securities regulator's approval of Unitree's roughly $619 million Shanghai IPO kept the humanoid sector's manufacturing-base race in the frame into the new week.
